004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024/**
025 * REST request <c>multipart/form-data</c> part annotation.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Identifies a method parameter on a {@link org.apache.juneau.http.remote.Remote}-proxy method (which must also be
029 * marked {@link org.apache.juneau.http.remote.Multipart @Multipart}) as a single part of a {@code multipart/form-data}
030 * request body &mdash; the Retrofit <c>@Part</c> pattern.  The engine adapts each {@code @Part} parameter into the
031 * streaming {@link org.apache.juneau.http.entity.MultipartBody}.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='topic'>Accepted part-source types</h5>
034 * <p>
035 * The bound argument type determines how the part body is produced:
036 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
037 *    <li>{@link CharSequence}/{@link String} (and other scalars such as numbers/booleans) &mdash; sent as a text field.
038 *    <li><c><jk>byte</jk>[]</c> &mdash; sent verbatim (repeatable).
039 *    <li>{@link java.io.File} &mdash; <b>streamed</b> from disk; the {@link #fileName()} defaults to the file's name.
040 *    <li>{@link java.io.InputStream} &mdash; <b>streamed</b> (one-shot; makes the request body non-repeatable).
041 *    <li>{@link java.io.Reader} &mdash; <b>streamed</b> as UTF-8 (one-shot; not drained during request building).
042 *    <li>An {@link org.apache.juneau.http.HttpBody} &mdash; used directly.
043 *    <li>Any other object (a bean) &mdash; serialized with the client's default serializer (e.g. JSON).
044 * </ul>
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * A <jk>null</jk> argument contributes no part.
048 *
049 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
050 * <p class='bjava'>
051 *    <ja>@RemotePost</ja>(<js>"/upload"</js>)
052 *    <ja>@Multipart</ja>
053 *    String upload(
054 *       <ja>@Part</ja>(<js>"title"</js>) String <jv>title</jv>,
055 *       <ja>@Part</ja>(name=<js>"attachment"</js>, fileName=<js>"report.pdf"</js>, contentType=<js>"application/pdf"</js>) <jk>byte</jk>[] <jv>data</jv>);
056 * </p>
057 *
058 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
059 *    <li class='note'>
060 *       Valid only on a method also annotated {@link org.apache.juneau.http.remote.Multipart @Multipart}; a
061 *       {@code @Part} parameter on a non-multipart method is rejected at proxy-build time.
062 *    <li class='note'>
063 *       Honored by the next-generation engine (<c>RestClient.remote(...)</c>) only; the classic engine currently
064 *       ignores this annotation.
065 * </ul>
066 *
067 * <p>
068 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
069 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
070 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
071 * (and possibly earlier).
072 *
073 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
074 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
075 * </ul>
076 *
077 * @since 9.2.1
078 */
079@Documented
080@Target(PARAMETER)
081@Retention(RUNTIME)
082@Inherited
083public @interface Part {
084
085   /**
086    * The part (form-field) name.
087    *
088    * <p>
089    * When blank, falls back to {@link #value()}, then to the reflected parameter name (requires the
090    * <c>-parameters</c> compiler flag).
091    *
092    * @return The annotation value.
093    */
094   String name() default "";
095
096   /**
097    * A synonym for {@link #name()}.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Allows shortened notation when only the part name is specified: <c><ja>@Part</ja>(<js>"title"</js>)</c>.
101    *
102    * @return The annotation value.
103    */
104   String value() default "";
105
106   /**
107    * The filename advertised in the part's {@code Content-Disposition} header.
108    *
109    * <p>
110    * When blank, no {@code filename} is emitted &mdash; except for a {@link java.io.File} argument, whose filename
111    * defaults to {@link java.io.File#getName()}.
112    *
113    * @return The annotation value.
114    */
115   String fileName() default "";
116
117   /**
118    * The per-part {@code Content-Type} (e.g. {@code "application/pdf"}, {@code "application/json"}).
119    *
120    * <p>
121    * When blank, a sensible default is used based on the argument type (e.g. {@code text/plain; charset=UTF-8} for
122    * text fields, the serializer's media type for beans, {@code application/octet-stream} for binary sources).
123    *
124    * @return The annotation value.
125    */
126   String contentType() default "";
127}
